pi_items.source: Task description line: 'Create two new folders named 'New-Topics' and 'Ongoing-Discussions''
Task description line: 'Create two new folders named 'New-Topics' and 'Ongoing-Discussions''
Task description: 'Create a text file at '~/Documents/new_topics_digest.txt'' (for user ga, ~ = /home/ga)
Task description: 'Draft an email to 'triage-team@company.com' with the subject 'Triage Priority List''

Email analysis of 50 ham corpus emails: 36 replies/forwards + 14 new topics. Maximum achievable reduction is 36 (moving all replies). Verifier expects <5 emails remaining (requiring 46+ emails moved out), but only 36 are replies.
setup_task.sh: 'Load 50 emails from the ham corpus' (loop: [ $IDX -ge 50 ] && break). Confirmed by email_count in /workspace/assets/emails/ham/
Parsed Subject headers from all 50 ham_*.eml files using regex pattern from verifier.py: '^\s*(\[.*?\]\s*)?(re|fw|fwd|aw|sv|vs)(\s*\[\d+\])?\s*:'

privileged_info_summary: This task uses real email data from the SpamAssassin ham corpus, not synthetic data. The inbox contains exactly 50 emails: 36 replies/forwards (72%) and 14 new topics (28%). The metadata specifies correct folder names ('New-Topics', 'Ongoing-Discussions'), correct digest file path ('/home/ga/Documents/new_topics_digest.txt'), and correct recipient email ('triage-team@company.com'). However, the metadata claim 'required_inbox_reduction: 45' appears incorrect—if all 36 replies are moved to 'Ongoing-Discussions' and all 14 new topics to 'New-Topics', only 36 emails are removed, leaving 14 in the inbox. The verifier expects fewer than 5 emails remaining for full credit, which would require moving 46+ out of 50 emails.
